capability: novelty-rate-sensitive reserve — surprise at a genuine newcomer tracks how often newcomers have RECENTLY arrived
claim: The reserve mass a reading holds for an as-yet-unseen atom should be signal-derived: a γ-decayed estimate of the recent newcomer rate, run through the fixed Born step. So a newcomer arriving after a long stretch of pure confirmation (rate→0, reserve→0) is MUCH more surprising than the identical-surface newcomer arriving in a churn of newcomers (rate high, reserve high). The fixed-constant reserve (1/(mass+1)) is BLIND to the rate — it scores the two newcomers ~equally. The dissociation is newcomer-specific: a repeat-final control shows no such gap under either reserve.
mechanism: signal-derived novelty reserve — noveltyReserveMass folds the γ-decayed first-appearance steps (a newcomer's-rate estimate under the SAME γ the field decays by) and feeds it as the `novelty` amplitude through the SAME fixed Born step (reserve = a/(sum+a)) inside surpriseAt. Context enters at the amplitude; the law stays put.

predictions.dissociation: for the newcomer probes, per sense: bayes_signal(settled) > bayes_signal(churning) by a wide margin (> 0.3 bits). bayes_fixed(settled) ~= bayes_fixed(churning) (|Δ| < 0.05): the fixed reserve is blind.
predictions.monotone_length: more confirmation deepens the settled effect: bayes_signal(settled, len15) >= bayes_signal(settled, len10); the churning side is rate-saturated and stays flat.
predictions.control: for the repeat-final controls the signal reserve produces NO wide settled-vs-churning gap (|Δ_signal| small, comparable to |Δ_fixed|) — the dissociation is specific to a newcomer, not a generic settled-vs-churning artifact.
predictions.loud_surface_control: both newcomer finals are equally unseen, so a cheap surface 'is-this-atom-new' detector fires identically on settled and churning and yields ZERO dissociation — exactly what the fixed reserve does. The settled>churning separation cannot come from surface novelty; it requires the rate-tracking reserve.
predictions.omnimodal: the text sense and the tone sense (a non-text, parser-free operator log) must show the SAME dissociation — the mechanism reads only the membrane.
